title: Consolidated Experimental Validation of the Universal Scaling Exponent β = 2/3: From Atomic Bonds to Cosmology

creators.name: Yakushev, Alexey V.
creators.affiliation: YUCT Research, Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory Core

description: This technical note consolidates experimental validations of the universal scaling exponent β = 2/3 ≈ 0.67 across physics, chemistry, biology, geophysics, and economics. More than fifteen independent datasets—including bond energies, phase transitions, metabolic scaling, earthquake statistics, superconducting currents, and economic volatility—all exhibit exponents consistent with 2/3 or its derived values. The combined probability of chance agreement is p < 10⁻²⁰. The document is part of the Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT) and demonstrates that β = 2/3 is a fundamental constant of nature. This record contains the complete multilingual version (English, Russian, German, Chinese, Arabic, Japanese, Korean).

structural_topology.description: This directory contains the multilingual versions (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) of the technical note 'Consolidated Experimental Validation of the Universal Scaling Exponent β = 2/3'. The English version is the original; all translations are full and equivalent in mathematical content, tables, and references.

custom.notes: The exponent β = 2/3 and its numerical value 0.67 are explicitly included in the keywords for discoverability. All empirical data, digitised sources, and analysis code are available in the YPSDC repository. The technical note is an integral part of the YUCT framework and relies on appendices C, C2, D, E, F, G, M, P, R, S, Ω, and the technical notes TN1–TN5.
